records.ety: [L. criminatus, p. p. of criminare, criminari, to criminate, fr. crimen. See Crime.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Criminated (-n<?/`t<?/d); p. pr. & vb. n. Criminating (-n<?/"t<?/ng).]

defs: To accuse of, or charge with, a crime.
quotes: To criminate, with the heavy and ungrounded charge of disloyalty and disaffection, an uncorrupt, independent, and reforming parliament.

defs: To involve in a crime or in its consequences; to render liable to a criminal charge.
quotes: Impelled by the strongest pressure of hope and fear to criminate him.
